Serotonin was the first to have server sided desync (super super op), not to mention the other exploits they have such as real time lighting, real time skybox changer (no other public external has this), gravity, local player mods. It literally has everything you need in an external. To ad d on, an amazing optimized lua environment that never crashes and has a bundle of functions, methods and properties.

Reviews.Author: 2c88dm0rwa
Reviews.Comment:
Overall, Serotonin is a great cheat with both its upsides and downsides, just like others. I take great liking to the visuals whilst others might focus on the aimbot or silent aim. The visuals primarily are the reason I still prefer Serotonin over Melatonin even though both of the developers are great at their jobs. I am a visuals-only type of cheater with very minimal aimbot usage, but that's not the only reason I use the cheat. 

Both developers, Auti and Fope are great and trustable people. Auti has done the Roblox, FiveM and R*st DMA whilst Fope only recently picked back up on the CS:2 External. Both of them are open to community suggestions and feedback, Auti confirming himself that he reads through suggestions and Fope asking the CS Beta team a lot for their opinions which is what a good developer should be doing. 

There is many great things about Serotonin, the cheat itself only being one of them. The community is generous with their giveaways and helpfulness (besides the sab idiots) and so are the developers, often hosting their own giveaways or media contests with bare minimum requirements. 200 views on any form of video with a month sub being the prize is more generous than any other developer I've seen. 

Pricing on the cheat is great too, only being 11$ for a month and getting exponentially cheaper with a year costing 54$, whilst others might've priced it at 70-90$. The caveat is crypto only payments, but resellers take most payments and are even cheaper. The only thing you should look out for when going to a reseller is checking if they're official or false advertising "Lifetime" as it isn't sold.

Categories.Comment:
Serotonin has an overall great aimbot with minor caveats such as smoothing being too exponential (10 smoothing doing very little nothing but 50 slowing it down to a great extent) and mouse being too smooth overall. These issues are obviously very minor and do not affect the cheats combat performance since they're easily solvable by switching aim styles or simply setting smoothing to 0. 
Auti, being the developer of the Roblox cheat, has listened to community feedback regarding mouse being too smooth and has increased the base speed but some people still think it requires more smoothing.

No comment on silent aim as I am not a Da Hood player nor do I use it on any game that uses mouse for its aim. Therefore I am unable to make a comment on it. Same applies for orbit and triggerbot, as my usage has been close to none. Community feedback on those two has had two major complains, one of them being the lack of triggerbot hitbox scaling and mouse spoofing being inconsistent across games. I am unable to verify mouse spoofing but can confirm triggerbot scaling missing.

The visuals are some of the best I've seen in a Roblox cheat, easily topping other providers I have used in the past including Matcha, DX9, Matrix, Isabelle and Severe. ESP visuals are clean and simple without much clutter to them, being easy to read and understand. The ESP has some features I haven't seen in other externals too, such as full mesh chams and its "Sonar" feature. The other cham modes work amazingly without a major impact on performance, rivaling on internal chams with how they look.

Other miscellaneous features the ESP has such as Serotonin User flag are neat and usually aren't seen in many providers both outside and inside the Roblox community. Customizability on elements is better than any Roblox provider (Besides Melatonin) can currently offer (from what i'm aware of.) offering a full 3D preview of your own Roblox avatar with draggable elements, allowing for many combinations such as HP bar on the top, name on the left, flags on the bottom and so on.

Serotonin offers many world modulation features too, ranging from the basics like brightness and fog to more advanced features like hiding the Roblox UI itself, real-time skybox alterring and even replacing the sun. There's even a feature for changing the actual lightning technology Roblox will use, offering 6 different modes. There is 9 world/lightning related categories offering endless possibilities for world and ESP combinations, this is several times more than the average provider will add to their own cheat. The majority of providers will only add fog, brightness and call it a day whilst Auti has gone out of his way to add over 50 different settings.

The UI for Serotonin is clean and minimalistic with slight improvements it may need. Some parts of the menu feel cluttered because of how many features it contains, a great example being the exploits tab and ESP settings. The menu itself is great in its looks because of the ability to theme nearly every part of it, instead of just the accent color like most cheats. I'd say the menu customizability borders Neverlose (CS:2 and CS:GO cheat) with just how many things there is to recolor.

Auti has done a great job on attempting to de-clutter the menu but if you are over-the-top with ESP like I am, it can be a struggle to find what you're looking for. The same applies for world modulation, if you are using too many features at the same time it can be a struggle to find what you need. Compared to the exploits and visual tabs, lighting is a lot more organized.

Auti is quick to update his cheat when it comes to Roblox updates, but isn't instant like most devs are. Whilst many other providers focus on updating their cheat just to earn more money and in the process break something without realizing it because of no prior testing, Auti focuses on making sure everything works before updating whilst balancing his personal life too. Updates take no longer than a couple of hours on a busy day but can be instant too.

Serotonin has many, many more features than every provider on the market. Nearly every developer on the market either focuses on updating their cheat purely for Roblox updates without adding any features every once in a while or simply fixing bugs. No provider will ever come close to the amount of customization Serotonin has without putting in many days and nights of work. Many of the Roblox cheat devs have even pasted features off Auti, such as Shadow-Clone Jutsu.

Let's take Matcha as an example. Their server-side desync is a shameless copy of Serotonin, even being paywalled to Beta users, in total costing 20$ just to use a buggy mess of an overall cheat. Vault didn't try to hide the fact he stole the feature since it is literally named "Shadow-Clone Jutsu", just like Serotonin. Serotonin being the first external to add a server-sided desync puts these other providers who need to copy the exact naming of the feature just to earn sales is pathetic. The first iteration of Shadow-Clone Jutsu was later leaked and patched shortly after, but Auti found yet another way to do it.

Reviews.Author: 1q5kwdiya2
Reviews.Comment:
the price that serotonin is being sold at is not really justified by anything
$10/mo is a bit much as for a set of basic features that are being carried by mesh chams

Categories.Comment:
All the basic stuff that can be found in most externals for roblox
The thing that stands out is visible/wallchek which works extremely well and so far i didn't see any external do it better than serotonin

Visuals are smooth and pretty much never laggy
Customizability is definately on the better side and mesh chams are one of the best currently avaliable especially considering that they are done externally 

On the downside a considerable amount of the visual options are added just for the show and there is barely any use for them
Mesh chams sometimes take quite a bit to parse and render all player parts on a fairly high end pc

Personally i'm a fan of modern looking uis so i'm a bit biased here
This type of design was and still is a bit overused in the entire community making taking away from the uniqueness of serotonin's ui

User experience is definately on the bright side although it desperately lacks descriptions for some options
another thing i could complain about is how scripts are loaded as i prefer more executor like ui instead of having scripts in a configs tab

Serotonin fakes their updates on weao and inject
Actual update gets pushed a few days later

Once again all the basic stuff that can be found in most other externals and even a little bit more than that

But the same issue as with visuals arises here
It feels like some features are added just to convince someone to buy the product
